_comment: EXAMPLE PLACEHOLDER CONFIG. Replace subscriptionId and ownerTag before running deploy.ps1. See README.md 'Things you must edit before running'.
_subscriptionId_comment: PLACEHOLDER. Replace with your Azure subscription ID (Get-AzContext or az account show --query id).
_ownerTag_comment: PLACEHOLDER. Replace with your name/email so resources are traceable to an owner.
_costCenterTag_comment: PLACEHOLDER. Replace with a cost center code meaningful to you/your org, or leave as-is for a personal sandbox.
_uniqueSuffix_comment: PLACEHOLDER. Generate a unique 6-character lowercase suffix before deploying, e.g. in PowerShell: -join ((97..122) | Get-Random -Count 6 | ForEach-Object {[char]$_}). Used to make the storage account and Key Vault names globally unique (e.g. stlzlite<uniqueSuffix>, kv-lzlite-<uniqueSuffix>).
_includeNetwork_comment: Default OFF. This is the only piece of the landing zone that meaningfully grows resource count. Set to true or pass -IncludeNetwork to deploy.ps1 to also create the spoke VNet/subnet/NSG.
_includeBudget_comment: Default ON. Azure Cost Management budgets and alerts are a free feature, so this is safe to leave enabled.
_tagPolicyName_comment: The tag name that the 'Inherit a tag from the resource group if missing' policy assignment will enforce inheritance for.
_rbacPrincipalObjectId_comment: PLACEHOLDER. Replace with a real Azure AD user/group/service principal object ID from your tenant before deploying if you want the Reader role assignment step to succeed. If left as-is or invalid, deploy.ps1 will log a warning and continue (non-fatal by design).
_contactEmail_comment: PLACEHOLDER. Replace with a real email address to receive budget alert notifications.
